text.title: Feature Flag Services, Testing, and Agentic Workflow Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the feature-flags-services-testing methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Every flag-gated code path has at least two pytest cases: ON and OFF.
rule.rationale: Tests covering only the active branch ship broken legacy paths; both branches must stay correct until cleanup.

rule.statement: CI runs a weekly job that fails when any flag has been live >90 days AND both branches are present in code.
rule.rationale: Stale flags rot into technical debt and confuse new contributors.

rule.statement: Prefer OpenFeature SDK when more than one runtime is involved (TS + Python + Java).
rule.rationale: OpenFeature abstracts away vendor; lets you swap providers without rewriting code.

rule.statement: Flag service must produce an audit log of who flipped what, when, and why (PR or ticket link).
rule.rationale: Without audit log, post-incident review is guesswork.

rule.statement: After a flag reaches 100% for 14 days, the flag agent opens a cleanup ticket assigned to the owner.
rule.rationale: Without a scheduled cleanup, no human will remember; agents close the loop.

rule.statement: Service choice is documented: OpenFeature, LaunchDarkly, GrowthBook, Statsig, or internal. Rationale tied to: SDK coverage, audit log, targeting UI, cost.
rule.rationale: Without a documented choice, teams ad-hoc adopt the wrong service.

rule.statement: If you have <3 flags or no CI capacity, skip this methodology and stay manual.
rule.rationale: Services + audits require flag mass to pay back.
